one_line_definition: LabVIEW program unit consisting of a Front Panel (GUI) and Block Diagram (code); can be nested as sub-VIs
LabVIEW's underlying dataflow programming paradigm in which data flows through wires connecting graphical function nodes
Intentionally sampling a bandpass signal below the Nyquist rate for the carrier frequency, exploiting aliasing to translate the signal to a lower IF while preserving information
Requirement that sample rate be at least twice the highest frequency in the signal to avoid aliasing
Efficient algorithm for the DFT requiring (N/2)log2(N) multiplications vs. N^2 for the DFT
Finite, discrete sampling of the continuous Fourier transform evaluating frequency content at N equally spaced bins
Energy from a frequency component not at a DFT bin center spreading into neighboring bins due to non-orthogonality
Multiplying the time-domain signal by a taper (Hann, Hamming, Blackman-Harris, etc.) to reduce spectral leakage at the cost of main-lobe width
Attenuation of a spectral component whose frequency falls between two DFT bin centers, caused by the shape of the implicit rectangular window
Digital filter with a finite number of coefficients; symmetric versions have linear phase, making them preferred in digital communications
Recursive digital filter with feedback; computationally efficient but introduces non-linear phase distortion
Processing signals at multiple sample rates within the same system, using interpolation and decimation filters
Efficient filter bank implementation obtained by decomposing a prototype filter into parallel branches operating at the lower rate
LabVIEW express VI that guides the user through a wizard to configure NI-DAQmx tasks and automatically generates the underlying VI code
National Instruments driver library providing functions for configuring, starting, reading, and clearing data acquisition tasks with NI hardware
IEEE 488.2-based standardized ASCII command language for remote control of test and measurement instruments

aliases_seen: General Purpose Interface Bus, IEEE 488, HP-IB
one_line_definition: Parallel digital bus standard originally developed by HP in the 1960s, still widely used in test and measurement for high-speed instrument control
Instrument I/O API layer abstracting serial, GPIB, USB, Ethernet, etc. connections under a common interface in LabVIEW
NI utility for discovering, configuring, and testing connected hardware and drivers; provides SCPI command console for direct instrument queries
NI binary file format for storing time-stamped measurement data; importable directly into Excel
LabVIEW cluster containing Status (Boolean), Code (integer), and Source (string) fields passed between functions to propagate and enforce error handling
LabVIEW debugging mode that slows VI execution and displays animated data values on wires as they are computed
LabVIEW debugging tool that pauses execution at a selected wire or function, enabling step-through inspection
Design pattern using a while loop + case structure driven by a shift register holding the current state; recommended for production-ready DAQ applications
LabVIEW architecture pattern where a loop waits for external user or hardware events before executing associated code blocks
Open-source microcontroller platform supported in LabVIEW via the LINX toolkit and VI Package Manager for low-cost DAQ and embedded control
Receiver that mixes RF down to an IF using an analog LO before ADC sampling; requires dedicated RF downconverter hardware
Receiver that samples the RF signal directly at a reduced rate exploiting bandpass sampling; eliminates the analog LO/mixer at the cost of SNR degradation
Digital modulation scheme encoding data in both amplitude and phase of a carrier; 16-QAM encodes 4 bits per symbol
Ratio of incorrectly received bits to total transmitted bits; key performance metric for digital communication systems
Measure of modulation quality comparing the actual transmitted symbol vector to the ideal reference vector
Modular instrument platform from NI based on CompactPCI; used for high-performance RF measurement (e.g., PXI-5660 RF signal analyzer)
